$comment: Endpoint/parameter contract for /demo-imagery-terrain.html (Imagery & Terrain Studio). The canonical server contract — base URL, glyphs, the three PMTiles raster archives (NAIP imagery, hillshade, terrarium terrain), the vector basemap style, and the dynamic ImageServer/terrain fallback routes — lives in assets/demo/layers.json and is consumed READ-ONLY by this page (same precedent as the Analyst Workbench). This file only adds what is specific to this demo: the STAC lanes, the elevation-profile parameters, and the static-vs-live latency race.

stac.$comment: Dual lane. The live demo server's /stac is a VALID STAC API with ZERO collections (verified 2026-06-12: GET /stac/collections → {"collections":[]}). The page probes collectionsPath on boot: non-empty → live lane (POST searchPath with searchBody); empty/unreachable → fixture lane, clearly labeled 'sample catalog — live STAC pending collections'. The fixture is a real STAC ItemCollection fetched at build time from Earth Search — see generate-stac-fixture.mjs and the honua:fixture block inside stac-items.json for provenance.

profile.$comment: Elevation profile parameters. Tiles come straight off the maui-terrain-static terrarium PMTiles archive (zooms 7-12, declared in layers.json) via byte-range reads — the SAME archive MapLibre drapes as 3D terrain; the page decodes pixels client-side: (R*256 + G + B/256) - 32768 metres. samples = points interpolated between the two picked locations; maxTiles caps how many distinct tiles one profile may touch (the sampler walks down from the archive's max zoom until under the cap).

latencyRace.$comment: Scene 3's static-vs-live contrast, run against our own server on user click only. Both routes serve the SAME West Maui hillshade tile: the static lane reads it out of the pre-baked PMTiles archive (HTTP range requests, CDN-cacheable, no database); the live lane asks the seeded ImageServer route to render it from PostGIS raster per request (kept seeded exactly to demonstrate dynamic rendering — layers.json documents it as the fallback lane). The z/y/x below is the {z}/{y}/{x} of the ImageServer tile route.

scenes.$comment: Camera presets per scene chip (narrative lives in studio.js next to each scene's code strip + capability list).
